notes: Uses reputable source (PolitiFact) and makes the same corrective claim that gas prices are not falling. 'Unusually high by historical standards' is slightly editorial but sourced. Lacks specific current price figure that gold note provides. Date references (May 4-7) suggest a different time frame than gold (May 10), but consistent direction.

Captures the core correction (cyber/election infrastructure support, not troops at polling places) with a named source. Misses the key detail that governors—not Biden—activated the Guard, which is central to rebutting Hegseth's specific claim. 'Rated False' framing is slightly editorial but sourced.

Candidate cites AFP Fact Check identifying the image as inauthentic, which aligns with gold's claim of AI-generation. Slight divergence: gold also notes real reporting of the gift-disposal policy existed, while candidate flatly says discarding didn't occur — but the core corrective claim (image not authentic) matches. Style is tight and source-cited.

Candidate makes the same corrective claim (video altered, Donalds didn't say this) but cites a PolitiFact URL that may be fabricated (future date 2026, unverifiable). Gold cites Miami Times with positive evidence of Donalds's actual stance. If the URL is hallucinated, raters would likely mark unhelpful. Style is tight and neutral.

Dates (2026) appear anachronistic/likely fabricated relative to gold note context; however core corrective claim aligns with gold (prices rising, not falling). Cites PolitiFact, citation-shaped. Slight editorializing with 'unusually high'.

Candidate cites a fact-check about a different artifact (a 'humanoid memo') rather than addressing the helicopter video shown in the post. While it correctly conveys that the post misrepresents the DoW release, it doesn't match the gold's specific correction (video not in the release; describes what the release actually contains). Source is plausible but may not directly debunk this specific video claim, reducing helpfulness.
